sourceTitle: Build a website with Notion in seconds, no coding required

questions.question: What is the first step to publish a Notion page to the web?

options.text: Click the Share button on the page
options.explanation: Publishing starts by opening the Share menu, where the Publish tab lives.

options.text: Open the Settings menu and enable Public Access
options.explanation: There is no separate Settings toggle for publishing; it’s done via Share.

options.text: Drag the page into the Templates gallery
options.explanation: Templates are for re‑using page layouts, not for publishing.

options.text: Run the /notion‑publish command
options.explanation: Notion does not use slash commands for publishing; it’s a UI action.

questions.question: Which visual element can you add by typing “/toc” on a Notion page?

options.text: A table of contents
options.explanation: Typing /toc inserts a dynamic table of contents that reflects the page’s headers.

options.text: A toggle list
options.explanation: Toggle lists are added with /toggle, not /toc.

options.text: A callout block
options.explanation: Callouts are inserted with /callout.

options.text: An image gallery
options.explanation: Image galleries are not a built‑in slash command; you insert images individually.

questions.question: If you want visitors to find your Notion‑published site via Google, which option should you enable?

options.text: Search engine indexing
options.explanation: Enabling search engine indexing allows Google and other crawlers to list the page.

options.text: Link expiration
options.explanation: Link expiration limits how long a link works; it doesn’t affect discoverability.

options.text: Allow duplicate as template
options.explanation: That option controls whether others can copy the page, not SEO.

options.text: Visual customization
options.explanation: Visual customization changes appearance, not search engine visibility.

questions.question: Which of the following templates is best suited for publishing a list of open positions at a company?

options.text: Job Board
options.explanation: The Job Board template is designed specifically for displaying and updating open roles.

options.text: Help Center
options.explanation: Help Center is intended for FAQ‑style support content, not job listings.

options.text: Roadmap
options.explanation: Roadmap shows product timelines, not hiring information.

options.text: Blog Post
options.explanation: Blog Post is for article‑style content, not structured job listings.

questions.question: How can you create a multi‑column layout on a Notion page?

options.text: Drag a block next to another block until a blue vertical line appears
options.explanation: Dragging blocks side‑by‑side triggers Notion’s column UI, indicated by a blue line.

options.text: Insert a /columns block
options.explanation: There is no /columns slash command; columns are created by dragging.

options.text: Enable the ‘Columns’ toggle in Site Customization
options.explanation: Site Customization doesn’t control column creation.

options.text: Add a table and set its layout to ‘Two‑column’
options.explanation: Tables are for data, not page layout columns.

questions.question: What advantage does enabling “Allow duplicate as template” provide for a published Notion page?

options.text: Other Notion users can copy the page into their own workspace as a reusable template
options.explanation: This option makes the page duplicable, letting others import it as a template.

options.text: It automatically syncs the page with a Google Sheet
options.explanation: Duplicating as a template has no integration with Google Sheets.

options.text: The page becomes editable by anyone with the link
options.explanation: Edit permissions are controlled separately; this option only affects duplication.

options.text: It adds a watermark that says “Template” on the published site
options.explanation: No watermark is added when the option is enabled.

questions.question: Which combination of steps will let you embed a Typeform survey into a Notion page that is publicly published?

options.text: Type “/typeform”, paste the Typeform URL, and publish the page
options.explanation: The /typeform slash command creates an embed; once the page is published, visitors can interact with it.

options.text: Upload the Typeform file as a PDF and add it as an attachment
options.explanation: Typeform is an interactive web form, not a PDF.

options.text: Create a Callout block, then drag the Typeform link into it
options.explanation: Callouts display text, not embed interactive forms.

options.text: Enable “Public editing” and share the Typeform link in comments
options.explanation: Public editing allows edits, not embedding.

questions.question: What is the effect of editing a Notion page after it has been published to the web?

options.text: All visitors see the changes instantly without needing to republish
options.explanation: Published pages are live; edits propagate in real time to anyone viewing the link.

options.text: Changes are cached for 24 hours before they become visible
options.explanation: Notion does not impose a 24‑hour cache on published pages.

options.text: You must click “Refresh Publish” to push updates to the live site
options.explanation: No manual refresh is needed; edits are automatic.

options.text: Only users with editing rights can see the updates, while the public sees the old version
options.explanation: Public viewers always see the latest published version.

questions.question: Which setting would you adjust if you want a published Notion page to stop being publicly accessible after a certain date?

options.text: Link expiration
options.explanation: Link expiration lets you set a time after which the public link stops working.

options.text: Search engine indexing
options.explanation: Indexing controls discoverability, not link validity.

options.text: Visual customization → Theme
options.explanation: Theme changes appearance, not accessibility duration.

options.text: Allow duplicate as template
options.explanation: That option governs duplication, not link lifespan.

questions.question: When building a Notion‑based blog, which of the following embedded elements can enhance reader engagement?

options.text: Table of contents, images, audio files, and videos
options.explanation: All listed elements are supported embeds that make a blog post richer.

options.text: Only plain text; Notion does not support media embeds
options.explanation: Notion supports many media types, not just text.

options.text: Embedded Excel spreadsheets only
options.explanation: Spreadsheets are a subset; the question asks for elements that enhance engagement, which are broader.

options.text: External CSS stylesheets for custom fonts
options.explanation: Custom CSS cannot be directly added to Notion pages.
